title: The Three Revenue Models
core_concept: All sustainable businesses use one of three models: Sell Time (1:1 coaching), Sell Product (digital courses/software), or Sell Systems (delegated execution). You can't mix time and scale.
insight: Most entrepreneurs get stuck because they try to serve everyone 1:1. The ceiling is your personal capacity (6 hours/week max). To 10x revenue, you must move from Time to Product or Systems.
quotes: You're trading time for money. That's the ceiling. $250K/year with 6 coaching clients, and you're burnt out.
Products scale. Systems scale. You don't. Pick one and own it completely.
The leap from $100K to $1M isn't more time. It's a different business model.
action_steps: Audit your current revenue: what % comes from Time, Product, Systems?
Pick your primary model (what you're best at)
Build systems to support that ONE model (don't hybrid)
case_studies: Sarah (Time): 6 clients × $250K = Burnt out, wants to scale
Marcus (Product): Digital course + community = Passive revenue
Jordan (Systems): Delegates 90% of execution, 10% strategy = Stress-free 7 figures

title: The Four Currencies Framework
core_concept: Money, Time, Energy, and Freedom are the real currencies. Most people chase money and lose Time/Energy/Freedom. True wealth is balancing all four.
insight: You don't optimize for dollars alone. You optimize for a lifestyle. 1-10 score yourself on each currency. The gaps show where you're out of balance.
quotes: Rich but exhausted? You optimized for money, sacrificed time and energy.
Freelance freedom? Sounds good until you're working 80 hours to stay alive.
The goal isn't to maximize money. It's to maximize freedom while keeping time and energy for what matters.
action_steps: Score yourself 1-10 on each currency: Money, Time, Energy, Freedom
Design ONE change to improve your lowest currency by 1 point
case_studies: Sarah: 8 Money, 2 Time, 3 Energy, 4 Freedom = Time is the blocker
Marcus: 4 Money, 7 Time, 6 Energy, 8 Freedom = Money is the blocker
Jordan: 9 Money, 8 Time, 7 Energy, 9 Freedom = Balanced, only energy slightly low

title: Finding Your Unfair Advantage
core_concept: Your unfair advantage is the intersection of what you're GOOD at, what you LOVE, and what people NEED. Everything else is busywork.
insight: Stop trying to be good at everything. You have maybe 2-3 things that are your actual superpowers. Double down on those. Hire or automate the rest.
quotes: Your unfair advantage isn't your degree. It's the 23 years of wisdom in your brain.
If 10 other people could do your job as well as you, it's not an advantage. It's commoditized.
Find the thing that would take someone else 10 years to replicate. That's your edge.
action_steps: List 5 things you're exceptional at (top 1% of people)
List 5 things people consistently pay you for
Find the intersection. That's your unfair advantage.
Everything else: delegate, outsource, or kill
case_studies: Tina: 23 years wisdom + direct teaching + community building = Unfair advantage in coaching
Marcus: Technical depth + business acumen + teaching ability = Unfair advantage in tech education
Jordan: Execution speed + pattern recognition + systems building = Unfair advantage in ops

title: Leverage: How to Stop Working
core_concept: Leverage is doing 1 hour of work that creates 10 hours of value. It's the only way to escape time-for-money. Tools, systems, people, products, and networks are leverage.
insight: Most people stay stuck because they trade time directly. Leverage breaks that. Your leverage unlocks your time.
quotes: You working 10 hours a week earning $250K? That's leverage. Your knowledge is the asset.
Without leverage, you hit a ceiling. With leverage, the ceiling is infinite.
The person who makes 10x more than you isn't 10x smarter. They have 10x more leverage.
action_steps: Identify your current leverage: tools, systems, people, products
Pick ONE type to increase (e.g., build a product, hire a VA, create a system)
Measure: How many hours of value per hour of effort?
case_studies: Sarah (1:1 coach): 1 hour coaching = 1 hour value. Needs leverage.
Marcus (course + coaching): 1 hour course = 100 students × benefit. High leverage.
Jordan (delegated system): 1 hour strategy = team executes 40 hours. Extreme leverage.

title: Value-Based Pricing (Not Time-Based)
core_concept: Price based on value delivered, not hours spent. A 30-minute decision that saves someone $100K is worth way more than a 10-hour project that delivers $2K.
insight: If you're pricing by the hour, you're leaving 10-100x money on the table. Value-based pricing aligns your incentives: you want to work fast and smart, not slow and long.
quotes: You charge $150/hour. That's your ceiling. Switch to value-based, and there's no ceiling.
$77/month for unlimited coaching from someone with 23 years of wisdom? That's massive value. Price accordingly.
Time-based pricing rewards inefficiency. Value-based pricing rewards results.
action_steps: Stop thinking in hours. Start thinking in outcomes.
Ask: What's the outcome worth to the customer? Price at 10-20% of that value.
Test: Raise your price 50%. What happens?
case_studies: Sarah: $150/hour × 10 hours = $1,500. Value-based: Client's problem worth $50K → Price: $7,500+
Marcus: Course with $100K/year outcome → Price: $1,000-$3,000 (value-based, not hourly)
Jordan: CFO work worth $500K/year in value → Charge $50-100K/year (10-20% of value)
